data.title: Banks cut oil price forecast to below $60 but market fundamentals seen rebounding
data.description:
More than two dozen banks have cut their 2025 forecasts for oil prices in the wake of OPEC's move to accelerate production hikes later this summer, according to a new survey by corporate law firm Haynes Boone.

The survey of 28 banks sees oil averaging $58.30/bbl this year, after the same banks projected 2025 oil prices averaging $61.89/bbl last fall.

The price drop is largely attributed to the increased production from OPEC+ and fairly stable demand levels, as well as the dramatic decline in prices seen in April following the Trump administration's tariff announcement.

While oil prices have shifted in the short term, the survey's long-term forecasts remain close to past projections in the $56-$57/bbl range in the early 2030s, suggesting the banks view recent economic changes as temporary.

Crude oil futures rose Thursday following news that the U.S. and China agreed to more trade talks following a phone call between President Trump and China's President Xi, sparking hopes for eventual increased oil demand expectations in both countries.

Escalating U.S.-China trade tensions have prompted fears of a recession and concerns over a softening of oil demand expectations, added by reports that Saudi Arabia is seeking another significant production hike.

Front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for July delivery ended +0.8% to $63.37/bbl, and front-month August Brent crude (CO1:COM) finished +0.7% to $65.34/bbl, the third gain in the past four sessions for both benchmarks, but front-month Nymex July natural gas (NG1:COM) closed -1% to $3.677/MMBtu.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

data.title: Greif's cost-cutting plan paying off, on track for $100M in savings by 2027, CEO tells WSJ
data.description:
Greif's (NYSE:GEF) strategy to streamline operations is paying off for now, positioning the company for significant growth when economic conditions improve, CEO Ole Rosgaard said Thursday, after reporting FQ2 adjusted earnings that topped estimates.

Q2 net income jumped 43% Y/Y to $68.7M, or $1.19/share, $0.06 better than the $1.13 analyst consensus estimate, and revenues edged 1.1% higher Y/Y to $1.39B, but missed the $1.42B analyst consensus.

Greif (NYSE:GEF) is making steady progress on its cost optimization program, Rosgaard told The Wall Street Journal, as the company expects to achieve up to $25M in savings by the end of the current fiscal year and remains on track to reach $100M in total savings by the end of FY 2027.

The company is pursuing deals in growing sectors such as pharmaceuticals, food and beverage, flavor and fragrance, and agricultural chemicals, the CEO said, noting that these are areas where improved performance is helping it gain market share and pass higher costs along to customers.

Greif (GEF) operates ~250 plants in more than 40 countries and sells most products locally, cushioning it from the effects of tariffs, but the company remains exposed to higher input costs for products such as steel, Rosgaard said.

data.title: Sunnova Energy slashes workforce by 55% as subsidiary files for bankruptcy
data.description:
Sunnova Energy (NYSE:NOVA) disclosed Thursday it plans to lay off 718 employees, or ~55% of its workforce, as it tries to cut costs while grappling with a massive debt pile and weak demand.

The disclosure comes days after subsidiary Sunnova TEP Developer filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection in Texas.

Previous reports have said Sunnova Energy (NYSE:NOVA) has been laying the groundwork for a potential bankruptcy filing that could occur in the coming weeks, and the Trump administration last week canceled a $2.9B partial loan guarantee that had been awarded to the company by the U.S. Department of Defense during the Biden administration.

A last-minute change to the budget bill passed by Congress last month would immediately end a 30% tax credit for solar leasing companies that help make rooftop systems affordable to homeowners, which the industry says would lead to a massive drop in the pace of installations.

ETF: (TAN)

data.title: Competing AI hyperscalers, Nebius and CoreWeave, make divergent moves
data.description:
Nebius Group (NASDAQ:NBIS) and CoreWeave (NASDAQ:CRWV), competing artificial intelligence hyperscalers, saw their respective stocks make divergent moves today.

Shares of Nebius surged 17% by the closing bell, while CoreWeave sank 17%.

Shares of both companies have risen rapidly over the past 30 days. Nebius has soared 90% over the past month, while CoreWeave has rocketed 165%. 

Arete Securities initiated coverage of Nebius today with a Buy rating and an $84 price target. The financial services firm pointed to the company's partnership with Nvidia (NVDA), $1.4B cash reserve and 400% year-over-year revenue growth.

Seeking Alpha analyst, The Asian Investor, also gave Nebius a Buy rating and named it a "top AI investment pick."

"The AI start-up has laid out an ambitious plan to grow its revenues aggressively through the scaling of its Data Center operations," The Asian Investor noted today. "In April, the company achieved approximately $310M in annual recurring revenue, showing 244% growth compared to the December 2024 ARR level. The AI start-up expects to grow its ARR business by at least 142% to a range of $750M to $1B by the end of the year."  

Nebius announced earlier this week that it is raising $1B in convertible notes to support its growth strategy in the AI market.

data.title: Dentsply Sirona prices offering of $550M of junior subordinated notes
data.description:
• Dentsply Sirona (NASDAQ:XRAY) on Thursday announced it has priced an offering of $550 million aggregate principal amount of its 8.375% Junior Subordinated Notes due 2055.
  • The offering is expected to close on June 12, 2025.
  • The notes will bear interest at a fixed rate of 8.375% per year until September 12, 2030.
  • Interest on the notes will be payable semi-annually in arrears on March 12 and September 12 of each year, commencing on September 12, 2025.
  • The net proceeds from the offering will be approximately $544.5 million.
  • The company intends to use the proceeds from the offering for general corporate purposes, including to repay indebtedness outstanding under the company’s existing 364-day $435 million term loan and other short-term indebtedness.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Gold forecast to hit new all-time high later this year, report says
data.description:
Gold likely will reach a new all-time high later this year on economic uncertainty stemming from U.S. policy, geopolitical tensions and strong central bank demand, precious metals consultancy Metals Focus said Wednesday in a new report.

Gold's average 2025 price is forecast to rise 35% to $3,210/oz, but a new high "will not be a smooth ride," Metals Focus said, as "sharp corrections and volatility are inevitable given speculator participation... We expect dips to be bought, reinforcing the uptrend."

Even if the worst of a potential global trade war is avoided, tariffs are still expected to be higher than in the past and so uncertainty is likely to persist, the report said.

Central banks worldwide are set to buy 1,000 metric tons of gold in 2025 for a fourth straight year of massive purchases, Metals Focus said, as they continue to shift reserves away from dollar assets.

"Diversification away from the U.S. dollar, whether from a portfolio or reserve management perspective, or for geopolitical reasons, remained the key driver" behind strong central bank demand, according to the report.

Gold futures turned lower in volatile trading Thursday after President Trump said he discussed trade in a call with China's President Xi, deflating some of gold's safe-haven appeal amid indications that the U.S. and China would enter deeper negotiations to settle their trade dispute.

Trump said on social media that the talks focused mostly on trade led and to "a very positive conclusion," while suggesting the export of rare earth minerals also had been discussed.

Gold had posted strong gains last week after Trump accused China of violating a temporary agreement with the U.S., threatening to reignite a trade war between the world's two largest economies.

Meanwhile, silver surged to its highest in 13 years, as investors broaden demand for the safety of precious metals beyond gold.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for June delivery closed -0.7% to $3,350.70/oz, its second loss in three sessions, but front-month June silver (XAGUSD:CUR) finished +3.4% at $35.689/oz, its highest settlement value since February 28, 2012.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: Hawthorn Bancshares announces new common stock repurchase program
data.description:
• Hawthorn Bancshares (NASDAQ:HWBK) announced on Thursday that its Board of Directors approved a new common stock repurchase program authorizing the repurchase of up to $10.0 million of the company’s common stock.
  • The new common stock repurchase program replaces the company’s prior common stock repurchase program.
  • The repurchased shares will be held in treasury and may be used by the company for general corporate purposes, including stock-based employee benefit plans and stock dividends.
  • It is expected that the stock repurchases will be funded by cash generated through cash on hand, operations and other sources, said the company, adding that, "At June 3, 2025, the company had 6,946,656 common shares outstanding."
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: NB Bancorp agrees to buy Provident Bancorp for $212M in stock, cash deal
data.description:
NB Bancorp (NASDAQ:NBBK) has agreed to acquire Provident Bancorp (NASDAQ:PVBC) in a stock and cash transaction that's expected to close in Q4 2025, the lenders said Thursday.

Based on NB's (NASDAQ:NBBK) stock price of $16.62 at the close of business on June 4, the deal is estimated to be valued at $211.8M.

Under the terms of the merger agreement, which was unanimously approved by both company boards, PVBC stockholders will receive for each share of PVBC common stock, at the holder's election, either 0.691 shares of NBBK common stock or $13.00 in cash, subject to allocation procedures to ensure that 50% of the shares of PVBC shares will receive the stock consideration.

NBBK is planning to issue about 5.9M of its common shares in conjunction with the merger, diluting the company's tangible book value by ~6.1%. The deal is expected to have an earn-back period of ~2.7 years.

As part of the merger, Joseph B. Reilly, president and CEO of Provident (NASDAQ:PVBC), will join the board of Needham Bank (NBBK).

The combined company will operate 18 branches across Metrowest, Greater Boston, the North Shore in Massachusetts and Southern New Hampshire. Total assets at transaction close are expected to be ~$7.1B, with $5.9Bin total deposits and $6.1B in total loans.

"This merger allows Needham Bank to expand into attractive market areas on the Massachusetts North Shore and in Southern New Hampshire where we already have a concentration of business clients," said Needham Bank Chairman, President and CEO Joseph P. Campanelli.

PVBC shares gained 4% in after-hours trading, while NBBK was little changed.
